signals.headline: Trump Orders Naval Blockade of Iran After Failed US-Iran Ceasefire Talks | NO | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
Trump Orders Naval Blockade of Iran After Failed US-Iran Ceasefire Talks | NO | HIGH confidence**

US-Iran negotiations in Islamabad collapsed on April 12, 2026, with no agreement on preventing Iran's nuclear development, as confirmed by Vice President JD Vance[1]. President Trump responded by ordering a naval blockade of Iranian ports within hours, aiming to counter Iran's blockade of the Strait
matched_markets: Will the US x Iran ceasefire be extended by April 21, 2026?
Trump announces end of military operations against Iran by A
Will Iran strike Iraq by April 30, 2026?

signals.headline: BANK OF KOREA HOLDS RATES AT 2
signals.raw:
BANK OF KOREA HOLDS RATES AT 2.5% AMID IRAN WAR INFLATION FEARS | NO rate change | HIGH confidence**

The **Bank of Korea kept its benchmark interest rate steady at 2.5%** on April 10, 2026, for the 7th consecutive month, with a unanimous decision among board members[5]. This decision maintains the status quo despite mounting inflationary pressures from the Iran conflict.
matched_markets: Will the US x Iran ceasefire be extended by April 21, 2026?
Will Iran strike Iraq by April 30, 2026?
Trump announces US x Iran ceasefire end by April 21, 2026?

signals.headline: **South Korea's March inflation rose to 2
signals.raw: **South Korea's March inflation rose to 2.2% year-on-year**, up from 2.0% in February but below the forecasted 2.4%[1][2]. Core inflation (excluding food and energy) declined to 2.2% in March from 2.3% in February[2].
matched_markets: Will South Korea’s 2026 Annual Inflation be between 2.7% and 2.9%?

signals.headline: **The BOK now projects inflation to "exceed considerably" its February forecast of 2
signals.raw: **The BOK now projects inflation to "exceed considerably" its February forecast of 2.2%** due to higher crude oil prices and won weakness, which have increased import costs[6]. The board expects inflation to reach the "mid to upper two-percent range"[5].

signals.headline: **Petroleum products surged 10
signals.raw: **Petroleum products surged 10.4% month-on-month in March**, reflecting oil price volatility from the Iran war[2]. South Korea, the world's fourth-largest oil importer, implemented nationwide fuel price caps for the first time in nearly 30 years[2].

signals.headline: **Analysts expect no rate changes for the remainder of 2026**, with the BOK's easing cycle considered complete[3][6]
signals.raw:
**Analysts expect no rate changes for the remainder of 2026**, with the BOK's easing cycle considered complete[3][6].

The decision signals the central bank prioritizes managing currency volatility and financial stability over addressing near-term inflation that remains above its 2% target.
